metadata.title: Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer in 2022: A Review for General Practitioners in Oncology.
metadata.text: Here, we present a contemporary review of treatment of non-small cell lung cancer in Canada based on current best practices.

metadata.title: Lung cancer: Biology and treatment options.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't, Review
metadata.text: Treatment options for lung cancer include surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, and targeted therapy.

metadata.title: Radiation treatment of lung cancer--patterns of practice in Canada.
metadata.study_type: Comparative Study, Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: Canadian practice in the treatment of lung cancers shows some variations although it is consistent with the trends in the literature.

metadata.title: Access to Cancer Drugs in Canada.
metadata.text: Lung cancer, because of the multiple subtypes now identifiable and because of the myriad of new and effective therapies, provides fertile ground to highlight issues related to oncology drug access in Canada [...].

metadata.title: Lung cancer: Biology and treatment options.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't, Review
metadata.text: The purpose of this review is to summarize the recent developments in lung cancer biology and its therapeutic strategies, and discuss the latest treatment advances including therapies currently under clinical investigation.

metadata.title: Radiation treatment of lung cancer--patterns of practice in Canada.
metadata.study_type: Comparative Study, Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: A questionnaire detailing different aspects of radiation treatment of lung cancer was mailed to all radiation oncologists treating lung cancer in Canada.

metadata.title: Evolution of systemic therapy for stages I-III non-metastatic non-small-cell lung cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Review
metadata.text: The treatment goal for patients with early-stage lung cancer is cure.

metadata.title: Demystifying the Role of Immuno PET-CT in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer: Clinical Value and Research Trends.
metadata.text: The management of Lung cancer, especially non-small cell lung cancer has undergone a paradigm shift recently with the advent of new treatment approaches including focused radiotherapy as well as evolution of a newer class of immunotherapy agents.

metadata.title: Immunotherapy for resectable lung cancer.
metadata.text: Lung cancer remains a significant global health challenge, demanding innovative treatment strategies.

metadata.title: Lung cancer for the non-respiratory physician.
metadata.text: Lung cancer treatment continues to develop with new surgical techniques, radiotherapy options and more drugs being licensed as part of standard treatment.

metadata.title: Lung cancer: current therapies and new targeted treatments.
metadata.text: In this Seminar, we discuss existing treatment for patients with lung cancer and the promise of precision medicine, with special emphasis on new targeted therapies.

metadata.title: The gut microbiome, immune check point inhibition and immune-related adverse events in non-small cell lung cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Review, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: Systemic treatment options for patients with lung cancer have expanded in recent years, with a number of immunotherapeutic strategies now in our treatment armamentarium.

metadata.title: Lung cancer: needs assessment, treatment and therapies.
metadata.text: This article will look at the latest national recommendations for managing and treating non-small cell lung cancer, which accounts for about 80% of lung cancers, as well as emerging therapies.

metadata.title: Developments in targeted therapy & immunotherapy-how non-small cell lung cancer management will change in the next decade: a narrative review.
metadata.text: The adoption of targeted therapy and immunotherapy has revolutionised the treatment landscape of non-small cell lung cancer.

metadata.title: Developments in targeted therapy & immunotherapy-how non-small cell lung cancer management will change in the next decade: a narrative review.
metadata.text: This review encompasses the latest updates in targeted therapy and immunotherapy in lung cancer management and discusses the future direction in the field.

metadata.title: Treatment strategies for malignant pulmonary nodule: beyond lobectomy. Point-counterpoint.
metadata.text: It has become a promising technique in lung cancer management with its diverse applicability.

metadata.title: Lung cancer staging: a concise update.
metadata.text: Diagnosis and clinical staging of lung cancer are fundamental to planning therapy.

metadata.title: Evolution of systemic therapy for stages I-III non-metastatic non-small-cell lung cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Review
metadata.text: In this Review, we present the current considerations in the treatment of patients with early-stage lung cancer and explore the current and future state of clinical research to develop systemic therapies for non-metastatic lung cancer.

metadata.title: What's new in thoracic oncology.
metadata.text: All these have significantly started to change our approach toward the management of lung cancer and have great potential to improve the lives of our patients afflicted with this disease.

metadata.title: New diagnostic and nonsurgical local treatment modalities for early stage lung cancer.
metadata.text: This paper highlights developments in diagnostic and nonsurgical local treatment modalities that have changed the management of early-stage lung cancer.

metadata.title: Developments in targeted therapy & immunotherapy-how non-small cell lung cancer management will change in the next decade: a narrative review.
metadata.text: We searched all original papers and reviews on targeted therapy and immunotherapy in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) using PubMed in June 2022.

metadata.title: New promises and challenges in the treatment of advanced non-small-cell lung cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Review, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: Targeted therapies and immunotherapies have radically improved treatment for advanced non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC).

metadata.title: S9900: Surgery With or Without Combination Chemotherapy in Treating Patients With Non-small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: PURPOSE: Randomized phase III trial to compare the effectiveness of surgery with or without combination chemotherapy in treating patients who have non-small cell lung cancer.

metadata.title: Association between treatment and improvements in overall survival of patients with advanced/metastatic non-small cell lung cancer since 2011: A study in the United States, Canada, and Germany using retrospective real-world databases.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: This study aimed to describe treatment patterns and overall survival (OS) in patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (aNSCLC) in three countries between 2011 and 2020.

metadata.title: Radiation Therapy in Treating Patients With Non-small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: PURPOSE: Phase I/II trial to study the effectiveness of radiation therapy in treating patients with non-small cell lung cancer that cannot be surgically removed.

metadata.title: Docetaxel or Pemetrexed With or Without Cetuximab in Patients With Recurrent or Progressive Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: This trial is a multicenter, open-label, randomized, Phase III study in patients with recurrent or progressive Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC) after failure of an initial platinum-based chemotherapy.

metadata.title: The Society of Thoracic Surgeons Expert Consensus on the Multidisciplinary Management and Resectability of Locally Advanced Non-small Cell Lung Cancer.
metadata.text: The contemporary management and resectability of locally advanced lung cancer are undergoing significant changes as new data emerge regarding immunotherapy and targeted treatments.

metadata.title: The biology and management of non-small cell lung cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't, Review
metadata.text: Important advancements in the treatment of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) have been achieved over the past two decades, increasing our understanding of the disease biology and mechanisms of tumour progression, and advancing early detection and multimodal care.

metadata.title: What's new in thoracic oncology.
metadata.text: In this review, we will discuss new diagnostic techniques for lung cancer, innovations in surgery, and major updates on latest treatment options including immunotherapy.

metadata.title: Review on endobronchial therapies-current status and future.
metadata.text: There is a growing demand for lung parenchymal-sparing localized therapies due to the rising incidence of multifocal lung cancers and the growing number of patients who cannot undergo surgery.

metadata.title: Advances in Targeted Therapy for Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Current Progress and Future Directions.
metadata.text: The advent of targeted therapies has significantly transformed the management of non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC), improving survival across all disease stages.

metadata.title: Recurrence-Free Survival in Patients With Surgically Resected Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer: A Systematic Literature Review and Meta-Analysis.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Systematic Review, Meta-Analysis, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: Standard treatment for early-stage or locoregionally advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) includes surgical resection.

metadata.title: Coformulation of Pembrolizumab/Vibostolimab (MK-7684A) Versus Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) Monotherapy for Programmed Cell Death 1 Ligand 1 (PD-L1) Positive Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (MK-7684A-003, KEYVIBE-003)
metadata.text: Researchers are looking for new ways to treat people with metastatic non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) that is PD-L1 positive.

metadata.title: Comprehensive Clinical Staging for Resectable Lung Cancer: Clinicopathological Correlations and the Role of Brain MRI.
metadata.text: CCS is effective for patients with resectable lung cancer, with less than 5% of patients being denied appropriate systemic treatment before surgery.

metadata.title: Maintenance Chemotherapy With or Without Local Consolidative Therapy in Treating Patients With Stage IV Non-small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: Giving maintenance chemotherapy and local consolidation therapy together may work better than maintenance chemotherapy alone in treating patients with stage IV non-small cell lung cancer.

metadata.title: The gut microbiome, immune check point inhibition and immune-related adverse events in non-small cell lung cancer.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Review, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: Toxicity of and resistance to treatment hold a major stake in lung cancer morbidity and mortality.

metadata.title: Role of Stereotactic Radiation Therapy in Operable and Inoperable Early-Stage Non-small Cell Lung Cancer.
metadata.text: Radiation therapy is becoming an increasingly important part of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) management.

metadata.title: Top advances of the year: Targeted therapy for lung cancer.
metadata.text: New strategies that combine multiple therapies are now being studied in randomized clinical trials, with the goal of enhancing the effectiveness of targeted therapy for patients with advanced lung cancer.
